subject: DevOps
title: Cloud IAM Best Practices
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Cloud IAM Best Practices.

hook.text: An S3 bucket said Allow star. It held 100 million records. One bad IAM policy exposed everything.

buildup.text: Cloud IAM controls who accesses what. A single misconfigured policy can expose your whole stack.

discovery.text: Least privilege: grant only what's needed. Use specific resource ARNs, not wildcards like star.

twist.text: Use IAM roles, not long-lived keys. Roles provide temporary credentials that expire on their own.

climax.text: IAM Access Analyzer, GCP Policy Analyzer, and Azure Advisor find overly permissive policies for you.

punchline.text: IAM is your cloud's front door. Lock it tight, audit it often.

quiz.question: Why are IAM roles preferred over long-lived access keys?
options: Roles provide temporary credentials that expire automatically
Roles are free while access keys cost money
Access keys provide more security than roles
